title: Stage 3, People Controls
subtitle: Answer all questions on this page. Every question is mandatory.

questions.text: Before employees or contractors join, do you conduct background checks (for example education, employment history, criminal records)?
questions.helpText: This checks whether you verify a person’s suitability before granting access to systems or sensitive information.

questions.text: Do employment contracts clearly define information security responsibilities for employees?
questions.helpText: Employment terms should make clear what employees are and are not allowed to do. For example, they must not share customer information with unauthorised people, reveal confidential business information to outsiders, use company systems for illegal activity, or disclose sensitive data such as financial records, passwords, or personal details.

controls.controlName: Information security awareness, education and training

questions.text: Do all employees receive regular information security awareness training (for example on policies, phishing, password security)?
questions.helpText: Training reduces human error and improves recognition of common attacks like phishing and social engineering.

questions.text: Do you have a formal disciplinary process for employees who violate information security policies?
questions.helpText: A formal process discourages policy violations and supports fair handling of incidents and misconduct.

questions.text: Is this process documented and communicated to all staff?
questions.helpText: Employees should know what happens when policies are violated, and managers should apply rules consistently.

controls.controlName: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment

questions.text: When an employee leaves or changes roles, do you have a defined process to remove their access and collect company assets?
questions.helpText: This reduces the risk of unauthorized access by ensuring accounts, devices, and credentials are handled promptly.

questions.text: Do all employees and relevant third parties sign confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements (NDAs)?
questions.helpText: NDAs help protect sensitive information and set clear expectations about disclosure and data handling.

questions.text: Are these agreements reviewed and updated regularly?
questions.helpText: Reviewing ensures the terms remain aligned with business changes, regulations, and new risks.

questions.text: When employees work remotely, do you have security measures in place (for example VPN, encryption, secure Wi-Fi guidance)?
questions.helpText: Remote controls reduce exposure from unsecured networks and unmanaged devices.

questions.text: Do you have clear, accessible channels for employees to report suspected security incidents or events?
questions.helpText: Fast reporting reduces impact by enabling early response and containment.

questions.text: Are employees trained on what to report and how to report it?
questions.helpText: Training ensures incidents are reported early, with the right details to support investigation.
